auth_notes: Requires an API Key generated within the Gingr application and the specific business subdomain for the request URL.
rate_limits: Not explicitly documented in public facing docs; typically follows standard SaaS throttling (approx 60-100 requests per minute).
gotchas: Endpoints are instance-specific based on the client's subdomain.
API v2 is the current standard, but older documentation for v1 may still exist and should be avoided.
Some advanced pet/owner metadata is stored in custom fields which require additional mapping logic.
research_notes: Integration will utilize the HTTP Request node. Authentication is straightforward via header-based API key. Webhooks are supported and can be configured in the Gingr admin dashboard to send POST requests to an n8n Webhook node for real-time event processing.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will utilize the HTTP Request node. Authentication is straightforward via header-based API key. Webhooks are supported and can be configured in the Gingr admin dashboard to send POST requests to an n8n Webhook node for real-time event processing.
